title: The care ladder counts appropriate referral behavior as a safety downgrade: 38% of all 'downgrades' are specialist→generalist moves
evidence: Numerators and structure confirmed, denominators off. Recomputed from patientwords/data/urgency_shift.json rows (flip_class=='downgrade'): 114 tier-3→2 rows confirmed exactly, but out of 315 downgrade rows = 36.2%, not 'of 301' (37.9%); gemma-2-2b: 47 tier-3→2 confirmed, but of 137 downgrades, not 123 (no current artifact yields 301/123; summary.flip_classes says 307). 3→2 is still the largest transition class ((3,2)=114, (1,0)=112, (2,1)=61). Tier ordering confirmed: urgency_shift.json tiers = {4:'emergency care',3:'specialist care',2:'generalist professional care',1:'self-treatment / OTC',0:'no care action / comfort / social'}; no clinical framework is cited in methods.html#step-4 or engine data/urgency_tiers.draft.json. provenance.json translation_cases.doctor_dermatologist confirmed: patient 'doctor' 0.677 (~68%) vs clinical 'dermatologist' 0.536 (~54%), and tier_examples put doctor=tier 2, dermatologist=tier 3, so it scores as a 3→2 downgrade. Target quotes verified: index.html:446 'the move is mostly down'; technical/index.html:385 'When the answer changes, it goes down the care ladder'. Anchoring premise directionally supported: among informative flips, clinical-side top is tier 1-3 in 577/654 rows and tier 4 in only 5.

title: Single ambiguous wordpieces decide downgrade calls, and the 'owner-reviewed v1' vocabulary contains self-contradictions including in the emergency tier
evidence: engine data/urgency_tiers.draft.json: 'blood' = {tier:2, note:'body part / symptom reference (not a care action)'} while tier 2 = 'generalist professional care' — the contradiction as stated; methods.html#step-4 tier table renders 'blood' as a tier-2 measured example at runtime (urgency_shift.json tier_examples['2']=['doctor','blood','prescription']). Tier 4 holds exactly 5 tokens ['emergency','er','hospital','ed','ers']; 'ed' and 'ers' both confidence:'review', note:'ER fragment/plural?'. 'sleep' = tier 1, note "'sleep (aid)' vs plain sleep - context split"; docs/tier_review_checklist.md line 6: 'Decides 3 call(s)' with three downgrade calls listed. Exactly 129 tokens have confidence='review' (recomputed). Checklist lines 30-31: 'new' blocks 23 flips, 'blood' blocks 12. Flip discard share confirmed to rounding: summary.flip_classes = uninformative 968 of 1587 flips (61.0%), downgrade 307 vs upgrade 53 (report's 967/1584/306 off by ≤3, share unchanged). Tiers do attach to bare lowercased tokens ('Tokens are lowercased bare continuations' per the file's own header).

title: Next-token story completion is framed as the model's 'recommendation', 'advice', and 'predicted action' — the exact reading the study's own limitations disclaim
evidence: All quoted copy verified verbatim. index.html:311 h1 'Medical AI must be ready to interpret them'; same phrase in og:description (line 13) and og:image:alt (line 18); index.html:313 'the competing recommendations ranked by probability'; index.html:441 heading 'Safety view — urgency of the predicted action'; methods.html:288-289 'The advice changed, not just its probability'; start-here/index.html:211 'When it changes toward less help… we count a downgrade'. Limitations counter-text confirmed at methods.html:512: 'That is a probe of model behavior, not a clinical outcome. A lower probability for “antacid” isn’t a measure of harm'. Stimuli are indeed third-person narrative completions (index.html:316-318 'at work I keep a spare ___'; methods.html:285-287 'Grandma’s been constipated… she took a'). The home safety view (index.html:439-448) contains the draft-tiers flag but no behavioral-probe/not-a-clinical-outcome scoping.

title: The 'LLM-written, not patient statements' disclosure is missing from precisely the pages where conclusions are presented
evidence: Core absence confirmed: the 'SIMULATED DATA · written by an LLM … not patient statements' endnote appears only at simulated-scenarios/index.html:476, simulated-scenarios/scenario.html:201, and wording-differences/index.html:290 (dialect-differences/index.html:307 has an equivalent LLM-authorship disclosure but without the literal SIMULATED DATA prefix). index.html endnote (line 487) says only 'are live gemma-2-2b traces'; translation endnote says 'The traces here are live gemma-2-2b runs.'; start-here's only stimulus caveats are 'simulated phrases' in the roadmap (line 358) and 'The clinician panel is illustrative, not a measurement.' (line 396), and its h1 (line 142) is 'People say the same thing many ways.' Correction: the claim that the home page contains no statement that the sentences are synthetic is overstated — index.html:429 states 'Claude writes new patient-vs-clinical pairs in the hand-built dataset’s format … automatic validators accept or reject each', with 'generated pairs · simulated data' (line 427) and hero cap 'scenario 85 of the simulated series' (line 351). That disclosure sits in the Simulated Scenarios gallery card, not in the hero demo, Figs. 1-4 captions, or the safety view; start-here and translation genuinely lack any stimulus-authorship statement.

title: Off-preregistration models are pooled into the 'claim-grade' table, and the most clinically salient claim (medgemma) rests on the weakest, exploratory evidence
evidence: engine docs/prereg_divergence_log.md line 12: 'llama-3.2-3b, olmo-2-1b, gemma-2-2b-it added; medgemma-4b-it probed 07-13' with 'extra models are secondary/exploratory; primary endpoints stay on the pre-registered four'. technical/index.html:340 'Eight models, one measurement.'; line 355 'Every band sits below zero.'; the claim-grade table (lines 392-411) has columns Model/Measurement/Circuit features/n phrases/Penalty/Down v up/q — no primary-vs-exploratory marker, and the row-building JS renders all 8 per_model entries identically (the only differentiated rendering, 'probe measured · full run in progress', applies to below_floor models, and data/model_stats.json below_floor=[]); models_meta carries no prereg flag. Endnote lines 601-602 call Part 4's cross-model statistics 'the claim-grade interim numbers'. data/model_stats.json per_model['medgemma-4b-it']: n_phrases=119, penalty mean -0.0339 (-3.4 pp), ci95 [-0.068,-0.002] = [-6.8,-0.2] pp (report wrote -6.9; trivial rounding difference), sign test 9 v 2, p_bh=0.07478 (q≈0.075, non-significant). methods.html:514 states flatly: 'The one medically tuned model measured, medgemma-4b-it (4B parameters, next-word behavior only), still shows a below-zero penalty.' — no n, CI, or exploratory label.
